a_exp_worldavg.reference: Fermilab Muon g-2 final, PRL 135 101802 (2025); BNL+FNAL world avg, 124 ppb

SM_WP2020_dispersive.reference: Theory Initiative White Paper 2020 (data-driven e+e- HVP)
SM_WP2020_dispersive.note: the dispersive baseline that produced the historical 4-5 sigma anomaly

SM_lattice_2025.reference: Theory Initiative White Paper 2025, arXiv:2505.21476, Phys.Rept. 1143 (2025) 1: a_mu(SM) = 116 592 033(62)e-11 (530 ppb), lattice-dominated HVP
SM_lattice_2025.note: lattice HVP; brings SM up, residual 38(63)e-11 ~ 0 ('no tension between SM and experiment at current precision' -- WP25 abstract)

SM_CMD3_related.note: CMD-3 raises the pi+pi- HVP toward the lattice; no single standardized CMD-3-only SM amu exists -> placeholder (data_limited)

SM_future_consensus.note: placeholder for a future consolidated SM HVP

kill_condition_dated.rule: if a data-driven (e+e-) HVP consensus emerges consistent with the lattice/WP25 value (CMD-3-side resolution), the TFPT g-2 bridge is DEAD: the residual pins to ~0.4e-9 and the 2.879e-9 seam-vertex reading sits at +3.9 sigma today, hardening past 5 sigma as HVP errors shrink toward the 127-ppb experimental precision. A KLOE-side dispersive consensus restores the bridge to < 1 sigma (+0.58 sigma on the WP2020 baseline).
watch: MUonE first results: Phase-1 pilot run completed at CERN SPS Jun-Aug 2025 (3 stations, ~5e11 scattering events); Delta-alpha_had analysis underway; full run post-LS3

new e+e- -> pi+pi- data: BaBar 2009+2025 combination (arXiv:2601.16587) keeps the dispersive prediction in tension with the FNAL measurement (KLOE-side); BESIII / Belle II / CMD-3 / SND remeasurements on 1-2 yr timescales; KLOE vs CMD-3 tension is >5 sigma at the rho peak
Theory Initiative WP-3, announced for ~2027-28 ('in 2-3 years', 8th g-2 workshop, Orsay Sep 2025): the next consensus SM HVP is the decision point

rare_kaons.kill_window_note: TFPT downstream-bridge kill window from the introduction; BR(K+) outside [7,12]e-11 would break the bridge

KL_pi0nunu_limit_90CL.reference: KOTO 2024 upper limit (90% CL); SM ~3e-11, far below current reach

haloscope_marker.note: 23.8 ueV (~5.76 GHz) inside the HAYSTAC/CAPP band but NOT excluded at DFSZ; exclusion status depends on the specific haloscope dataset and coupling model

DM.AXION.HILLTOP.01.note: full finite-T solve overcloses DM by ~5.5x unless dilution; observed level needs theta_i ~ 106 deg

DM.AXION.SPINE.01.note: FROZEN before any run: theta_i = 3pi/5 from the spine quotient N_fam/g_car = 3/5; full finite-T solve (fa=Mscal/128, NDW=1) lives in experiments/ftransfer/axion_relic; not a prediction of record
